summary: Gate exit code was 0, but Phase 1 acceptance/spec requirements are still not met: pytest does not run (missing module + gate masks pipeline failures), Deal API wiring/contract probe/tools use /api/deals instead of decision-locked /deals, transition_deal is probe-only and not approval-enforced, checkpoint encryption is not integrated with PostgresSaver (no SecurePostgresSaver; kill-9 is simulated), Langfuse is unreachable but treated as optional (soak passes with 60 Langfuse failures), PII canary gate is simulated/fail-open, and backend/Agent API port remains 8091 instead of decision-locked 8095. These are recurring from Cycle 1 → potential stuck loop.

blockers.title: Pytest not executed; phase1 gate masks failures
blockers.details: gate_artifacts/phase1/pytest_output.log contains '/usr/bin/python3: No module named pytest', yet scripts/gates/phase1_gate.sh reports 'pytest: PASSED'. The pytest gate is marked optional (scripts/gates/phase1_gate.sh:109 uses required=false) and is run via a pipeline with tail as the last command without 'set -o pipefail' (scripts/gates/phase1_gate.sh:30), so the exit code can be 0 even when pytest fails to start. Acceptance requires 'pytest tests/' to pass and the gate to exit nonzero on failure.
blockers.expected: Pytest is installed, tests run, and phase1_gate exits nonzero if pytest is missing or any tests fail.
blockers.actual: Pytest is missing and tests do not run, but phase1_gate still reports PASSED and exits 0.
blockers.recommended_fix: Make pytest a required gate; add 'set -euo pipefail' (or capture PIPESTATUS) and remove/avoid piping pytest into tail. Ensure the gate runs in an environment with project deps (e.g., venv/uv or docker).
validation_steps: ./scripts/gates/phase1_gate.sh (confirm it fails when pytest is missing or tests fail)

blockers.title: Deal API endpoints use /api/deals instead of decision-locked /deals
blockers.details: Authoritative docs require Deal tools to map to GET /deals, GET /deals/{id}, POST /deals/{id}/transition (/home/zaks/bookkeeping/docs/ZakOps-Ultimate-Implementation-Roadmap-combine.md §3.3). However, the client, probe, and tool gate all call /api/deals (e.g., src/core/agent/deal_api_client.py:340, scripts/probes/deal_api_contract_probe.py:65, scripts/gates/tests/test_deal_tools.py:102). Artifacts confirm /api paths (gate_artifacts/phase1/deal_api_contract.json required_endpoints.path='/api/deals*', and deal_api_* artifacts show api_endpoint='GET /api/deals'). The probe output strings are misleading (prints '/deals' while probing '/api').
blockers.expected: All three deal tools and the contract probe use the decision-locked endpoints: /deals, /deals/{id}, /deals/{id}/transition.
blockers.actual: Implementation and artifacts use /api/deals* and the probe misreports endpoint strings.
blockers.recommended_fix: Update Deal API client/tools/probe/tests to use /deals* paths per Decision Lock. Ensure the probe records and prints the actual tested paths (no mismatched strings).

blockers.title: transition_deal is not approval-enforced and gate proof is probe-only
blockers.details: CRITICAL tools must require approval per Decision Lock. src/core/agent/deal_tools.py only logs a warning when approval_id is missing (src/core/agent/deal_tools.py:289-298) and still calls the Deal API. The gate artifact gate_artifacts/phase1/deal_api_transition_deal.json shows probe_mode=true with HTTP 422 and explicitly states 'no actual transition', which does not meet acceptance requiring transition_deal to call the real Deal API after approval.
blockers.expected: transition_deal refuses to execute without an approval_id and the Phase 1 gate proves an approved transition (non-probe) against the real Deal API.
blockers.actual: transition_deal can execute without approval_id and the gate only performs a probe request (no approved transition).
blockers.recommended_fix: Enforce approval_id (fail closed) in transition_deal_tool and add an E2E gate that runs invoke→approval→transition and verifies a real stage change (HTTP 200/201).
validation_steps: Run transition_deal_tool without approval_id and confirm it returns an approval-required error and makes no HTTP call
Run the HITL approval flow and confirm the transition endpoint succeeds and artifacts reflect a real transition

blockers.title: Checkpoint encryption not integrated with PostgresSaver (SecurePostgresSaver missing); kill-9 test is simulated
blockers.details: Decision Lock §3.6 requires checkpoint encryption via a SecurePostgresSaver wrapper covering checkpoint_blobs.blob and checkpoint_writes.channel_values. src/core/security/checkpoint_encryption.py claims to provide SecurePostgresSaver (src/core/security/checkpoint_encryption.py:5) but defines only SecureCheckpointEncryptor (no SecurePostgresSaver class). scripts/gates/tests/test_encryption.py only verifies in-memory encrypt/decrypt (scripts/gates/tests/test_encryption.py:26-41). scripts/gates/kill9_encrypted_test.sh explicitly simulates crash recovery (scripts/gates/kill9_encrypted_test.sh:235-270) rather than kill -9 + resume with Postgres-backed checkpoints. The artifacts therefore do not prove ciphertext-at-rest in Postgres nor real resume.
blockers.expected: Encrypted checkpoints are persisted in Postgres (ciphertext at rest) and kill -9 restart resumes from encrypted checkpoints using SecurePostgresSaver.
blockers.actual: Encryption is only verified in-memory; SecurePostgresSaver integration is missing and kill-9 recovery is simulated.
blockers.recommended_fix: Implement SecurePostgresSaver and wire it into the real checkpointing layer. Update encryption/kill9 gates to create real checkpoints in Postgres, kill the process, restart, and verify both resume correctness and ciphertext in DB columns.
validation_steps: Run kill9_encrypted_test.sh and verify it actually kills/restarts the agent process
Query Postgres for checkpoint blob columns and confirm they are encrypted (ENC:v1 prefix; no plaintext)

blockers.title: Langfuse is unreachable but treated as optional; soak passes despite 100% Langfuse failures
blockers.details: gate_artifacts/phase1/langfuse_health.json shows healthy=false (unreachable). scripts/gates/phase1_gate.sh marks Langfuse optional (scripts/gates/phase1_gate.sh:167 required=false), so Phase 1 can PASS with Langfuse down. Additionally, scripts/gates/soak_24h.sh treats Langfuse failures as non-critical (scripts/gates/soak_24h.sh:137-140) and gate_artifacts/phase1/soak_smoke.json reports failures_by_service.langfuse=60 with verdict PASS. Acceptance requires Langfuse reachable at :3001 and health check 200.
blockers.expected: Phase 1 gate fails until Langfuse is reachable and healthy; soak treats Langfuse health as required (or the gate fails earlier).
blockers.actual: Langfuse is unreachable, but the gate is SKIPPED and soak still returns PASS.
blockers.recommended_fix: Make Langfuse gate required and ensure Langfuse is started for gate runs (or fail closed). Treat Langfuse failures as failures in soak if Langfuse is required by Phase 1.
validation_steps: ./scripts/gates/phase1_gate.sh (confirm langfuse gate fails when service is down)

blockers.title: PII canary gate is simulated/fail-open and cannot prove canary absence in logs/traces/DB
blockers.details: scripts/gates/pii_canary_gate.sh does not inject the canary into a real workflow (scripts/gates/pii_canary_gate.sh:121-125 'Simulated injection'). Database scanning is skipped when psql is unavailable (scripts/gates/pii_canary_gate.sh:85-88) but the gate still passes. The generated report gate_artifacts/phase1/pii_canary_report.json claims PASS despite these skipped checks and even contains an empty-string entry in 'violations', reducing trust in the artifact. Acceptance requires proving the canary does NOT appear in logs/traces/DB.
blockers.expected: Canary is injected into an actual agent run and the gate fails closed if it cannot scan logs/traces/DB; report accurately lists scanned locations and violations=[] when none.
blockers.actual: Injection is simulated; DB scan may be skipped; the gate still returns PASS and report contains a spurious empty violation entry.
blockers.recommended_fix: Invoke the agent with the canary payload, export/scan Langfuse traces and logs, and query Postgres using psql or a Python DB client. Fail the gate if any required surface cannot be scanned. Fix report JSON generation for empty violations.
validation_steps: Run pii_canary_gate.sh with Langfuse and Postgres available and confirm it scans all three surfaces
Intentionally place the canary in a log/trace/DB row and confirm the gate fails

blockers.title: Agent API port is 8091, not decision-locked 8095
blockers.details: Decision Lock fixes Agent API at :8095 (/home/zaks/bookkeeping/docs/DECISION-LOCK-FILE.md Quick Reference). This repo's docker-compose exposes backend on 8091 (docker-compose.yml:73) and phase1 gate/soak default to http://localhost:8091 (scripts/gates/phase1_gate.sh:37; gate_artifacts/phase1/soak_smoke.json configuration.backend_url). This violates spec/acceptance requiring Agent API on 8095.
blockers.expected: Agent API is served and checked on port 8095.
blockers.recommended_fix: Change backend service and gate defaults to port 8095 (or add a dedicated Agent API service on 8095) and update health checks accordingly.

majors.title: Deal tools and probe gates are fail-open (can PASS without real API/artifacts)
majors.details: scripts/gates/phase1_gate.sh marks deal_api_probe optional (scripts/gates/phase1_gate.sh:120 required=false). scripts/gates/tests/test_deal_tools.py prints warnings when Deal API calls fail but still exits 0 (scripts/gates/tests/test_deal_tools.py:252-263), so the phase1 gate can pass without proving real Deal API connectivity or artifact generation. This undermines deterministic gate requirements and acceptance criteria that tools call the real Deal API and produce artifacts.
repro_steps: Stop Deal API service on :8090 (so requests fail)
Observe deal_tools can still report PASS despite WARN/no artifacts
majors.expected: Deal API probe and deal_tools gate fail if Deal API is unreachable or required artifacts are not produced.
majors.actual: deal_api_probe is optional and deal_tools test exits 0 even after WARNs and zero artifacts.
majors.recommended_fix: Make deal_api_probe required and update test_deal_tools.py to treat missing artifacts / non-200 responses as failures. Remove fail-open behavior.
validation_steps: Bring Deal API down and confirm phase1 gate fails
Bring Deal API up and confirm artifacts are created and gate passes

majors.title: docker-compose includes default secrets for Langfuse/Postgres
majors.details: docker-compose.yml hardcodes default credentials via env var fallbacks, including POSTGRES_PASSWORD default 'zakops_dev' (docker-compose.yml:17) and Langfuse NEXTAUTH_SECRET/SALT defaults (docker-compose.yml:132-133). Mission requires env-based secrets and 'no default secrets' in production mode. At minimum, production mode should fail closed when these are not set to non-default values.
majors.expected: Secrets are provided via environment without insecure defaults, and production fails closed if unset.
majors.actual: Defaults are present for Postgres password and Langfuse secrets.
majors.recommended_fix: Remove default secret fallbacks or gate them behind explicit development mode. Add startup checks that reject default values in production.
validation_steps: Run with ENVIRONMENT=production and unset secrets; confirm services refuse to start or gate fails

minors.title: Deprecated datetime.utcnow() used in Deal API contract probe
minors.details: scripts/probes/deal_api_contract_probe.py uses datetime.utcnow() (scripts/probes/deal_api_contract_probe.py:202), emitting a DeprecationWarning in gate output. This is low risk but noisy.
minors.expected: Use timezone-aware UTC timestamps without deprecation warnings.
validation_steps: Re-run the probe and confirm no DeprecationWarning is emitted

minors.title: PII canary report includes empty-string violation entry
minors.details: gate_artifacts/phase1/pii_canary_report.json contains violations: [''] even when verdict is PASS. This likely comes from scripts/gates/pii_canary_gate.sh building VIOLATIONS_JSON from an empty array (scripts/gates/pii_canary_gate.sh:231-233).
minors.expected: violations should be [] when none are found.
minors.actual: violations contains an empty string entry.
minors.recommended_fix: Only run the jq pipeline when VIOLATIONS has entries; otherwise set VIOLATIONS_JSON to [].
validation_steps: Re-run pii_canary_gate.sh and confirm violations is an empty array when no violations are found

next_actions_for_builder.summary: Fix phase1 gate to be strict/deterministic (pipefail, required gates, pytest actually runs) and update backend URL/port to decision-locked :8095.
how_to_validate: Run `./scripts/gates/phase1_gate.sh` and confirm it fails when pytest is missing/failing

next_actions_for_builder.summary: Align Deal API wiring/probe/tests/artifacts to decision-locked endpoints `GET /deals`, `GET /deals/{id}`, `POST /deals/{id}/transition` (no /api prefix) and ensure probe output matches tested paths.
how_to_validate: Run `python3 scripts/probes/deal_api_contract_probe.py` and verify `deal_api_contract.json` required_endpoints paths are `/deals*`
Run phase1 gate and verify `deal_api_*.json` artifacts show `/deals*` endpoints

next_actions_for_builder.summary: Implement `SecurePostgresSaver` and wire checkpoint encryption into real Postgres-backed checkpoints; update encryption/kill9 gates to verify ciphertext-at-rest in DB and real resume after kill -9.
how_to_validate: Run `bash scripts/gates/kill9_encrypted_test.sh` and confirm it performs an actual kill -9 + restart
Query Postgres checkpoint tables/columns and confirm encrypted blobs (ENC:v1 prefix) with no plaintext

next_actions_for_builder.summary: Enforce HITL approval for `transition_deal` (fail closed without approval_id) and add an E2E proof that an approved transition succeeds against real Deal API.
how_to_validate: Call transition without approval and confirm it is blocked
Run invoke→approve→transition flow and confirm HTTP 200/201 and stage change

next_actions_for_builder.summary: Make Langfuse required: ensure docker-compose brings it up on :3001, gate fails if unhealthy, and produce a real trace proof artifact with redacted content.
how_to_validate: Run phase1 gate and verify `langfuse_health.json` has healthy=true and trace proof includes an actual trace id

next_actions_for_builder.summary: Replace simulated PII canary gate with real injection + scanning of logs/traces/DB and fail closed if any surface cannot be scanned; fix report generation.
how_to_validate: Run `bash scripts/gates/pii_canary_gate.sh` and confirm it performs real agent invocation and DB/traces scanning
Verify `pii_canary_report.json` has violations=[] and includes explicit scanned trace/log/DB locations

evidence: scripts/gates/phase1_gate.sh:109 runs pytest with required=false and pipes to tail without pipefail (scripts/gates/phase1_gate.sh:30)
scripts/gates/pii_canary_gate.sh:121-125 simulated canary injection; scripts/gates/pii_canary_gate.sh:85-88 skip DB scan when psql missing
scripts/gates/kill9_encrypted_test.sh:235-236 explicitly simulates kill-9 (no real process kill/restart)
